layman_headline: Fear grips the market — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin dominance high at 56.5%, meaning money is flowing away from smaller coins and into Bitcoin. While several coins have pulled back sharply, none of them are showing the kind of clean reversal setup that gives us confidence to enter. Ethereum is the strongest large-cap candidate with RSI approaching oversold territory, but it hasn't yet reached a compelling support level for a low-risk entry. We're waiting for a better price or a clearer reversal signal before committing capital.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines for now. Set alerts for Ethereum near $1,800 — that's where support sits and where a buy could make sense. Don't chase anything that's already bounced.
expert_reasoning: Scanning all candidates: BONK has strongly bearish chart structure (-2.0 bias) with Three Black Crows — catching that knife is reckless despite oversold RSI. BUILDon (B) is up 102% in 7 days with no chart data — pure FOMO, no edge. LDO has a bullish chart (+0.85) but terrible R/R (0.38x) with resistance at $0.40 just 7% away and RSI already at 59. PUMP is up 32% in a week in a downtrend — extended. The BinanceLife token has unclear fundamentals and mismatched price data. ETH stands out with RSI at 39 (our highest-confidence signal type at 80% win rate), but the chart bias is still -0.70 with price below all major MAs. The 5.5% weekly bounce means we may have already missed the best entry. The prudent move is to wait for ETH to pull back to $1,800 support or show a confirmed reversal pattern before entering. High BTC dominance (56.5%) is an active headwind for all altcoin positions.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% actively suppresses altcoin performance — could continue rising
Fear & Greed at 29 is improving but hasn't bottomed convincingly — further downside possible
ETH chart structure remains bearish with price below all major moving averages
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with volume confirmation would be the entry trigger
BTC dominance trend — any reversal below 55% would significantly improve alt entry conditions
Fear & Greed index — a dip below 20 (extreme fear) followed by reversal often marks optimal swing entries
market_context: The market is in a fear phase (F&G 29) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.5%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. BTC itself is showing mild strength (+3% weekly) but isn't dragging alts along — classic risk-off rotation within crypto.
context_banner: Market fear is improving but hasn't capitulated — discipline means waiting for cleaner setups rather than forcing trades into headwinds.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our highest-confidence signal type (80% win rate) — but hasn't yet triggered below 30 for a strong entry.
Fear & Greed at 29 improving from 25.9 weekly average suggests sentiment is bottoming, but hasn't reached extreme fear levels that mark the best swing entries.
ETH derivatives reset coverage suggests leveraged positions are being cleared, which typically precedes cleaner directional moves.
Peter Brandt's bear market timing call and Strategy's continued Bitcoin accumulation reinforce BTC-centric sentiment, keeping alt headwinds in place.
No volume breakout signals detected across candidates — no conviction moves to trade, reinforcing the HOLD decision.
